title: eryngium globosum
intro: Eryngium globosum, also known as the Globe Sea Holly, is a perennial flowering plant in the carrot family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It is a low-growing, thistle-like plant with blue or purple flowers and a rosette of spiny, grey-green leaves. The Globe Sea Holly has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and reducing swelling. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever and promoting digestive health.
